senses.definition: plural of pressuriser, a device that adds pressure to a system or container, especially to maintain or regulate pressure.
examples: The aircraft used two pressurisers to keep cabin pressure stable at high altitudes.
Repair technicians checked the pressure readings from the pressurisers.

senses.definition: to cause something to become pressurized or to apply pressure to a container or system.
examples: The system pressurises the tank to maintain fuel flow.
He pressurises the beaker by sealing it and pumping air in.

senses.definition: the act of making or becoming pressurized; the process of applying pressure to a system or container.
examples: The crew is pressurising the cabin to keep air inside.
Continuous pressurising can wear out the seals.

senses.definition: the process of adding pressure to a system or container; also the state of being pressurized.
examples: Cabin pressurization is essential for high-altitude flight.
A fault in the pressurization system can cause headaches and dizziness.

senses.definition: plural of pressurization; multiple instances or systems where pressure is added or maintained.
examples: The aircraft has two redundant pressurizations for safety.
Maintenance checked the pressurizations of all cabin sections.

senses.definition: to increase the pressure inside a container or system; to force someone to accept or experience pressure or intimidation.
examples: We need to pressurize the tank slowly to avoid rupture.
The manager tried to pressurize the team into finishing early.

senses.definition: held at a higher internal pressure than the surrounding environment; under pressure or emotionally stressed.
examples: The pressurized cabin kept air comfortable during the flight.
He spoke in a pressurized voice, trying to hide his anxiety.

senses.definition: a device or component that increases or maintains pressure in a system, especially in engineering or spacecraft.
examples: The pressurizer failed, causing the cabin to lose pressure.
A backup pressurizer ensures safety during ascent.

senses.definition: plural of pressurizer; devices that increase or regulate pressure in systems.
examples: Redundant pressurizers are installed along the pipeline.
The system uses two pressurizers to keep pressure steady.

senses.definition: to cause to become pressurized or to apply pressure to a container or system.
examples: The system pressurizes the chamber to keep it dry.
They pressurize the lines to prevent leaks.

senses.definition: the act or process of making something pressurized.
examples: The crew is pressurizing the tank before launch.
Pressurizing the system helps maintain consistent flow.

senses.definition: a woman who works as a journalist or reporter for the press.
examples: The presswoman reported live from the scene.
She became a respected presswoman in the national newsroom.

senses.definition: plural of presswoman; women who work as journalists or reporters.
examples: The presswomen gathered for a panel discussion.
Several presswomen covered the press conference.

senses.definition: work related to the press or printing, or activities of the press in journalism.
examples: The archive contains years of presswork from the era.
Training in presswork is essential for aspiring reporters.

senses.definition: a person who works for the press or printing industry.
examples: The pressworker printed the latest edition.
Pressworkers joined a union to negotiate wages.

senses.definition: the facilities or factories where presswork or printing is carried out; the operations of a press.
examples: The pressworks shut down for maintenance.
New pressworks were opened to handle demand.

senses.definition: an archaic or literary term meaning readiness, preparedness, or a state of being ready; in some uses, a form of respect.
examples: In an old text, the warrior stood in prest for battle.
With prest thoughts, she prepared for the journey.

senses.definition: an obsolete or rare term referring to the state of being prestabilized; stabilization in advance.
examples: The treatise discusses prestabilism as a theoretical concept.
Prestabilism is rarely used in modern texts.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being prestable; capable of being prepared or stabilized.
examples: Prestability is discussed in early technical writings.
The concept of prestability influenced subsequent design.

senses.definition: capable of being prestable or prepared in advance; able to be stabilized.
examples: The prevalidated model was prestable for the test.
We looked for prestable options in the design.

senses.definition: an early or pre-printed stamp, or a stamp that is already affixed before use.
examples: The letter had a prestamp indicating it was processed.
Collectors seek prestamps from rare issues.

senses.definition: having a stamp already affixed before mailing or processing.
examples: The envelope was prestamped and ready to mail.
Prestamped labels simplified the process.

senses.definition: the act of applying a stamp before use; the process of stamping in advance.
examples: Prestamping speeded up mail handling.
Prestamping was common in the early 20th century.

senses.definition: to stamp something in advance, especially mail or packaging.
examples: The company prestamps all outgoing letters.
Prestamps reduce in-person processing time.

senses.definition: a standard that exists before an official standard is set; an interim or draft standard.
examples: The prestandard guided early product development.
Regulators reviewed the prestandard before final approval.
